data.body: Consideration: [Security] _direct_rest_put() MUST reuse the same credential injection mechanism that the existing AcliClient uses (via _build_env / ACLI subprocess env). Do NOT independently read JIRA_URL / JIRA_USER / JIRA_API_TOKEN or construct new auth headers — duplication invites divergence (e.g., one path respects rotated credentials; the other caches them). Unit tests should mock the header-injection layer and assert that both _direct_rest_put and _run_acli share the same source of truth. This is the first time credentials will be used in-process (acli-integration.py previously delegated to the acli binary) — split-brain credential handling is a classic silent-auth-failure source. [adversarial-review, blue-team accepted]
